title: FunctionAttrs: Basic propagation of nofpclass
author: arsenm
description:
Perform caller->callee propagation of nofpclass on callsites. As
far as I can tell the only prior callsite to callee propagation here
was for norecurse. This doesn't handle transitive callers.

I was hoping to avoid doing this, and instead get attributor/attributor-light
enabled in the default pass pipeline. nofpclass propagation enabled by
default is the main blocker for eliminating the finite_only_opt global
check in device-libs, but this single level of propagation is most likely
sufficient for that use. Implemnting this here is probably the most expedient
path to removing the control library.

tests.test_body:
declare hidden float @merged_arg_uses(float, half, half)

define float @calls_merged_arg_uses(float %arg0, half %arg1, half %arg2) {
  %call0 = call float @merged_arg_uses(float nofpclass(nan inf) %arg0, half nofpclass(zero) %arg1, half nofpclass(zero) %arg2)
  %call1 = call float @merged_arg_uses(float nofpclass(nan) %arg0, half nofpclass(sub) %arg1, half %arg2)
  %ret = fadd float %call0, %call1
  ret float %ret
}

define hidden float @merged_arg_uses(float %arg0, half %arg1, half %arg2) {
  %fpext0 = fpext half %arg1 to float
  %fpext1 = fpext half %arg1 to float
  %add0 = fadd float %arg0, %fpext0
  %add1 = fadd float %add0, %fpext1
  ret float %add1
}

declare hidden float @merged_ret_uses(ptr)

define float @calls_merge_rets(ptr %ptr) {
  %call0 = call nofpclass(nan inf) float @merged_ret_uses(ptr %ptr)
  %call1 = call nofpclass(nan zero) float @merged_ret_uses(ptr %ptr)
  %add = fadd float %call0, %call1
  ret float %add
}

declare float @return_f32_extern(ptr)

declare hidden float @only_noinf_ret_uses(ptr)

define float @calls_no_inf_return(ptr %ptr) {
  %call0 = call float @return_f32_extern(ptr %ptr)
  %call1 = call nofpclass(inf) float @only_noinf_ret_uses(ptr %ptr)
  %add = fadd float %call0, %call1
  ret float %add
}

define hidden { double, double } @mixed_ret_and_args(float %scalar.arg, i32 %not.fp, <2 x double> %vec, { double, double } %struct) {
  %fpext = fpext float %scalar.arg to double
  %vec.0 = extractelement <2 x double> %vec, i32 0
  %insert.0 = insertvalue { double, double } poison, double %fpext, 0
  %insert.1 = insertvalue { double, double } poison, double %vec.0, 0
  ret { double, double } %insert.1
}

comments.author: mikaelholmen
comments.body:
Hello @arsenm 

The following starts failing with this patch:
```opt -verify-each -passes="cgscc(inline),rpo-function-attrs" bbi-115351.ll -S -o /dev/null```
Result:
```
Attribute 'nofpclass(all)' applied to incompatible type!
ptr @safe_add_func_int64_t_s_s
LLVM ERROR: Broken module found after pass "ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass", compilation aborted!
```
[bbi-115351.ll.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25468694/bbi-115351.ll.gz)

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
> Hello @arsenm
> 
> The following starts failing with this patch: `opt -verify-each -passes="cgscc(inline),rpo-function-attrs" bbi-115351.ll -S -o /dev/null` Result:
> 
> ```
> Attribute 'nofpclass(all)' applied to incompatible type!
> ptr @safe_add_func_int64_t_s_s
> LLVM ERROR: Broken module found after pass "ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass", compilation aborted!
> ```
> 
> [bbi-115351.ll.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25468694/bbi-115351.ll.gz)

I think this is an inline or pass manager bug when there is a call in unreachable code. If you first run cgscc(inline), then separately run rpo-function-attrs, it doesn't happen. This code shouldn't need to worry about seeing a function with no uses.

> > LLVM ERROR: Broken module found after pass "ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass", compilation aborted!

> I think this is an inline or pass manager bug when there is a call in unreachable code. If you first run cgscc(inline), then separately run rpo-function-attrs, it doesn't happen. This code shouldn't need to worry about seeing a function with no uses.

I wrote an issue about it:
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/182834

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: @arsenm The call graph is allowed to have spurious ref edges.

comments.author: alexfh
comments.body:
@arsenm we're seeing a miscompilation with `-Os` after this PR: https://gcc.godbolt.org/z/E9qbsnojK

Could you take a look? Thanks!

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
> Could you take a look? Thanks!

I do see a suspicious change, which wasn't actually triggered by this PR. In `@_ZNK3Foo3barEv`,   `%sub = fsub double %call2, %call4` is replaced by `%sub = fsub nnan double 0.000000e+00, %call4`.  Not sure why, nofpclass didn't even propagate to the return value

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
> Does reverting [a80d432](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/a80d4329ce96856a02bd279c800c3d08619da4c9) help?

Opened https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/183835 for this
